annotations.label: Definition of 1000

anchor.snippet: 1000 or one thousand is the natural number following 999 and preceding 1001

annotations.note: The natural-number literal `(1000 : ℕ)` exists via `Nat`/`OfNat`, but Mathlib has no dedicated declaration for 1000 or for the predecessor/successor characterization.

annotations.label: Chiliad

anchor.snippet: A group of one thousand units is sometimes known, from Ancient Greek , as a chiliad

annotations.note: This is a linguistic/etymological term with no mathematical content to formalize.

annotations.label: Millennium

anchor.snippet: A period of one thousand years may be known as a chiliad or, more often from Latin , as a millennium

annotations.note: Calendar/etymology terminology; not a mathematical statement.

annotations.label: Short thousand / long thousand

anchor.snippet: described as a short thousand in medieval contexts where it is necessary to distinguish the Germanic concept of 1200 as a long thousand

annotations.note: Historical/linguistic distinction with no Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: 1000 is the first 4-digit integer
annotations.note: Trivially provable from decimal-digit length but not recorded as a named theorem in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Decimal representation of one thousand

anchor.snippet: The decimal representation for one thousand is 1000 —a one followed by three zeros

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Nat.digits` but no specific lemma recording the decimal digits of 1000.

annotations.label: SI prefix kilo-

anchor.snippet: The SI prefix for a thousand units is " kilo- ", abbreviated to "k"—for instance, a kilogram or "kg" is a thousand grams

annotations.note: SI metrology is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kilo- in computer science (2^10)

anchor.snippet: In computer science , however, "kilo-" is used more loosely to mean 2 to the 10th power

annotations.note: Convention about a unit prefix, not a mathematical theorem to formalize.

annotations.label: Non-breaking space as thousands separator

anchor.snippet: a non-breaking space can be used as a thousands separator

annotations.note: Typographic convention, no Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: K/k abbreviation for thousands

anchor.snippet: Multiples of thousands are occasionally represented by replacing their last three zeros with the letter "K" or "k"

annotations.note: Informal abbreviation, not mathematical content.

annotations.label: Grand (colloquial for 1000)

anchor.snippet: A thousand units of currency , especially dollars or pounds , are colloquially called a grand

annotations.note: Slang term for currency; not a mathematical statement.

annotations.label: Chiliagon
annotations.note: Grep finds no chiliagon-specific declaration; general regular polygons are not parameterized by edge count under a named lemma here.

annotations.label: 1009 is the smallest four-digit prime
annotations.note: Mathlib has `Nat.Prime` but no named claim that 1009 is the smallest four-digit prime.

annotations.label: 1023 = sum of five consecutive primes
annotations.note: No Mathlib lemma asserts that 1023 = 193+197+199+211+223 or a related identity.

annotations.label: 1024 as smallest 4-digit square

anchor.snippet: 1024 is the smallest 4-digit square and also a Friedman number

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Nat.sqrt` and `IsSquare` but no lemma identifying 1024 as the smallest 4-digit square or as a Friedman number.

annotations.label: 1027 = sum of squares of first eight primes

anchor.snippet: 1027 = sum of the squares of the first eight primes

annotations.note: No specific Mathlib lemma encodes this identity.

annotations.label: 1079 = bound for tenth-power sums

anchor.snippet: 1079 = every positive integer is the sum of at most 1079 tenth powers

annotations.note: Waring's problem upper bounds for tenth powers are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1089 reversal property

anchor.snippet: first natural number whose digits in its decimal representation get reversed when multiplied by 9

annotations.note: No lemma about digit-reversal under multiplication by 9 in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1093 is the smallest Wieferich prime
annotations.note: Grep for `Wieferich` in Mathlib returns no results.

annotations.label: 1105 as Carmichael number and sum of two squares in four ways
annotations.note: Mathlib's `ArithmeticFunction.carmichael` is the Carmichael (reduced totient) function, not Carmichael numbers; no Carmichael-number predicate exists.

annotations.label: 1124 Leyland number
annotations.note: Grep for `Leyland` in Mathlib returns nothing.

annotations.label: 1128 dimensional rep of largest VOA with central charge 24

anchor.snippet: 1128 is the dimensional representation of the largest vertex operator algebra with central charge of 24

annotations.note: Vertex operator algebras and their representation dimensions are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1140 tetrahedral number
annotations.note: Grep for `tetrahedral` returns no Mathlib results.

annotations.label: 1155 product of first four odd primes

anchor.snippet: product of first four odd primes

annotations.note: No named lemma about 1155 = 3·5·7·11 in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1163 smallest prime > 34²
annotations.note: No specific lemma about the smallest prime exceeding 34² in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1184/1210 amicable pair
annotations.note: Grep for `amicable` returns no Mathlib results.

annotations.label: 1200 long thousand reckoning

anchor.snippet: 1200 = the long thousand , ten " long hundreds " of 120 each, the traditional reckoning of large numbers in Germanic languages

annotations.note: Historical/linguistic, not a mathematical statement.

annotations.label: 1210 self-descriptive number
annotations.note: Self-descriptive numbers are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1225 = 35² is triangular, square-triangular, hexagonal
annotations.note: Mathlib has no `triangularNumber`/`hexagonalNumber` predicate or square-triangular characterization.

annotations.label: 1229 = number of primes under 10,000
annotations.note: Mathlib has `Nat.primeCounting` (π function) but no lemma evaluating π(10000) = 1229.

annotations.label: 1260 highly composite, smallest vampire number
annotations.note: Grep finds no `highly composite` or `vampire` definitions in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1275 = 50th triangular number / sum of first 50 naturals

anchor.snippet: equivalently the sum of the first 50 natural numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Finset.sum_range_id` giving ∑ k in range n, k = n*(n-1)/2 from which the value 1275 follows, but no instance lemma names 1275. (Moderated: corrected decl name from `Gauss.sum_range_id`.)

annotations.label: 1279 Mersenne prime exponent
annotations.note: Mathlib defines `mersenne` and the Lucas–Lehmer test certifies particular Mersenne primes, but I found no recorded `Nat.Prime (mersenne 1279)` certificate.

annotations.label: 1296 = 36² = 6⁴ and rectangles on a chessboard

anchor.snippet: sum of the cubes of the first eight positive integers, the number of rectangles on a normal 8 × 8 chessboard

annotations.note: No specific lemma identifies 1296 as ∑ cubes or as rectangle count of an 8×8 grid.

annotations.label: 1306 digit-power identity

anchor.snippet: raising the digits of 1306 to powers of successive integers equals itself

annotations.note: Munchausen-style digit-power identities are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1325 Markov number
annotations.note: Grep for `Markov` in `Mathlib/NumberTheory` returns no Markov-number definition.

annotations.label: 1331 = 11³, unique non-trivial cube x²+x−1

anchor.snippet: This is the only non-trivial cube of the form

annotations.note: This Mordell-curve type result (only nontrivial cube of form x²+x-1) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1361 third Mills' prime
annotations.note: Grep for `Mills` returns no Mills-constant or Mills-prime declaration.

annotations.label: 1364 Lucas number
annotations.note: Mathlib has Lucas–Lehmer and Lucas-of-Kummer but no Lucas-number sequence Lₙ = Lₙ₋₁ + Lₙ₋₂.

annotations.label: 1365 pentatope number
annotations.note: Grep for `pentatope` returns nothing in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1366 Arima number and Chinese Rings puzzle

anchor.snippet: Arima number, after Yoriyuki Arima who in 1769 constructed this sequence as the number of moves of the outer ring in the optimal solution for the Chinese Rings puzzle

annotations.note: Arima/Chinese-Rings analysis is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1387 fifth Fermat pseudoprime base 2
annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Nat.FermatPsp` and proves infinitude, but does not enumerate pseudoprimes nor record that 1387 is the fifth.

annotations.label: 1409 smallest n whose 8th power is sum of 8 eighth powers

anchor.snippet: smallest number whose eighth power is the sum of 8 eighth powers

annotations.note: No Mathlib lemma about minimal sums of eighth powers.

annotations.label: 1430 Catalan number
annotations.note: Mathlib defines `catalan` with the recursion and explicit binomial formula, but does not record `catalan 8 = 1430` as a named lemma.

annotations.label: 1444 smallest pandigital in Roman numerals

anchor.snippet: smallest pandigital number in Roman numerals

annotations.note: Roman-numeral pandigitality is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1458 max determinant 11×11 0/1 matrix

anchor.snippet: 1458 = maximum determinant of an 11 by 11 matrix of zeroes and ones

annotations.note: Mathlib has determinant definitions but no Hadamard-style bound for 0/1 matrices of size 11.

annotations.label: 1459 Pierpont prime / sum of nine consecutive primes
annotations.note: Grep for `Pierpont` returns nothing in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1487 safe prime
annotations.note: Grep for `SafePrime`/`safe_prime` returns no Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: 1540 triangular, hexagonal, decagonal, tetrahedral
annotations.note: Mathlib lacks dedicated triangular/hexagonal/decagonal/tetrahedral-number predicates.

annotations.label: 1543 prime dividing all Fibonacci sequences
annotations.note: Generalized-Fibonacci divisor properties for 1543 are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1556 sum of squares of first nine primes

anchor.snippet: 1556 = sum of the squares of the first nine primes

annotations.note: No named lemma in Mathlib for this digit-trivia identity.

annotations.label: 1585 Riordan number
annotations.note: Grep for `Riordan` returns no Mathlib results.

annotations.label: 1597 Fibonacci prime / Markov prime
annotations.note: `Nat.fib` is defined in Mathlib and `fib 17 = 1597` is computable, but the assertion that 1597 is a Fibonacci prime is not a named lemma.

annotations.label: 1600 = 40²
annotations.note: Mathlib does not record the specific identity 40² = 1600 nor the rhombicosidodecahedral-number concept.

annotations.label: 1634 smallest four-digit narcissistic number

anchor.snippet: 1634 = the smallest four-digit Narcissistic number in base 10

annotations.note: Grep for `narcissistic` returns nothing in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1666 largest efficient pandigital in Roman numerals

anchor.snippet: 1666 = largest efficient pandigital number in Roman numerals

annotations.note: Roman-numeral pandigitality is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1669 smallest prime with gap 24 to next prime

anchor.snippet: smallest prime with a gap of exactly 24 to the next prime

annotations.note: Mathlib's prime-gap library does not record the smallest prime with gap 24.

annotations.label: 1680 highly composite
annotations.note: No `HighlyComposite` predicate found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1681 = 41², smallest non-prime in n²+n+41

anchor.snippet: smallest number yielded by the formula

annotations.note: Mathlib has no lemma about Euler's prime-generating polynomial n²+n+41.

annotations.label: 1691 strobogrammatic number

anchor.snippet: the same upside down, which makes it a strobogrammatic number

annotations.note: Strobogrammatic numbers are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1728 = 12³ great gross / cubic inches in cubic foot

anchor.snippet: the cube of twelve (called a great gross ), and so, the number of cubic inches in a cubic foot

annotations.note: Mathlib does not record 12³ = 1728 as a named lemma nor any imperial-unit conversions.

annotations.label: 1729 taxicab/Hardy-Ramanujan number

anchor.snippet: 1729 = taxicab number , Carmichael number, Zeisel number, centered cube number, Hardy–Ramanujan number

annotations.note: Grep for `taxicab`, `Hardy.?Ramanujan`, `Zeisel`, and Carmichael-number predicates all return nothing relevant in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1733 Sophie Germain prime
annotations.note: Grep finds `Sophie-Germain identity` (algebraic) in `Algebra.Ring.Identities` but no Sophie Germain prime predicate.

annotations.label: 1760 yards in a mile

anchor.snippet: 1760 = the number of yards in a mile

annotations.note: Imperial-unit conversions are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1771 tetrahedral number
annotations.note: No tetrahedral-number definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1791 largest natural not sum of at most four hexagonal numbers

anchor.snippet: 1791 = largest natural number that cannot be expressed as a sum of at most four hexagonal numbers

annotations.note: Hexagonal-number sums (Fermat polygonal-number theorem case) are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1792 Granville number
annotations.note: Grep for `Granville` finds nothing in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1801 cuban prime / sums of consecutive primes
annotations.note: Cuban primes (primes of form (x³ - y³)/(x - y)) are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1806 Sylvester / primary pseudoperfect / Bernoulli denominator

anchor.snippet: product of first four terms of Sylvester's sequence , primary pseudoperfect number

annotations.note: Grep finds no `Sylvester sequence` or pseudoperfect-number definitions in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1807 fifth Sylvester term
annotations.note: Sylvester's sequence is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1828 meandric number and digits of e

anchor.snippet: appears twice in the first 10 decimal digits of e

annotations.note: Meandric numbers and decimal-digits-of-e claims are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1834 octahedral / sum of cubes of first five primes
annotations.note: Octahedral numbers and the specific cube-of-primes identity are not formalized.

annotations.label: 1836 proton-to-electron mass ratio

anchor.snippet: 1836 = factor by which a proton is more massive than an electron

annotations.note: Physical constants are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1849 = 43², palindromic in base 6
annotations.note: Mathlib lacks a palindromic-digits predicate as a named lemma instance.

annotations.label: 1855 rencontres number
annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes derangements (the k=0 column of the rencontres table) but does not have rencontres D(n,k) more generally nor the specific value 1855.

annotations.label: 1858 alkanes C14H30 count
annotations.note: Chemical-isomer enumeration is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1900 primes ≤ 2^14
annotations.note: Mathlib has `Nat.primeCounting` but no lemma evaluating π(2^14) = 1900.

annotations.label: 1909 hyperperfect number
annotations.note: Hyperperfect numbers are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1936 = 44² 18-gonal
annotations.note: 18-gonal (octakaidecagonal) numbers are not formalized; the bare identity 44² = 1936 is not a named Mathlib lemma.

annotations.label: 1973 Sophie Germain / Leonardo prime
annotations.note: Sophie Germain primes and Leonardo numbers are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1980 highly abundant

anchor.snippet: highly abundant number with a greater sum of proper divisors than all smaller numbers

annotations.note: `Nat.sigma` exists in Mathlib but no `HighlyAbundant` predicate; specific instance not recorded.

annotations.label: 1984 nonunitary perfect number
annotations.note: Nonunitary perfect numbers are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1987 300th prime number
annotations.note: Mathlib has `Nat.nth Nat.Prime` but no recorded evaluation that the 300th prime is 1987.

annotations.label: 135 primes between 1000 and 2000

anchor.snippet: There are 135 prime numbers between 1000 and 2000

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Nat.primeCounting` but no lemma evaluating it on this interval to 135.
